breakthroughs.title: hexa → fake-cublas end-to-end LINK + EXECUTE
breakthroughs.summary: Minimal /tmp/firstlight.hexa (26 LOC) exercising extern fn + @link(cudart/cublas) + println successfully: (1) codegen'd via ./hexa self/build_c.hexa in ~1s to 10873-byte self-contained C, (2) linked by clang against /tmp/fake_cublas/{libcudart,libcublas}.dylib (NO-OP stubs from B5), (3) produced 33696-byte binary, (4) executed with rc=0, emitting expected cudaSetDevice rc=0 + cudaDeviceSynchronize rc=0 output.

stdout: [firstlight] LINK OK (execution is fake, NO-OP only)

chain_proven: hexa extern fn declaration → C extern int declaration
hexa fn main() → hexa_user_main() called from int main
build_c.hexa emits self-contained .c (inline runtime — NO need for separate self/runtime.c)
clang link resolves cuda symbols against fake dylibs
macOS arm64 binary executes + returns rc=0
side_findings: B1 agent's /tmp/codegen_smoke_full.hexa (6315 LOC bundle embedding lexer+parser+codegen_c2) hung 9h 27m @ 82% CPU. Root cause: double interpreter layering — outer ./hexa parses the 6315-LOC bundle, then the bundle's codegen_c2_full runs O(N^2) string concat on the 61KB flat AST. Killed PID 69126.
./hexa interpreted build_c.hexa tokenize scales super-linearly: 4KB=1s, 10KB=27s, 61KB>180s (extrapolated ~17 min). Production pipeline needs build_c.hexa precompiled to native.
scripts/build_train_gpu_c.hexa driver script has two bugs (see next_steps_priority #6).
